behaviors.description: Error message names missing required key by exact config path (e.g., 'database.host')
behaviors.justification: Tests string formatting and nested dict key lookup. No filesystem or external services involved. Mocking file I/O to return a parsed YAML dict and mocking os.environ are sufficient to verify error messages correctly name missing keys with dotted paths.
behaviors.test_description: Mock YAML file loading to return a config dict missing a required key (e.g., missing database.host). Mock environment variables to return absent values for corresponding TOOL_* env var. Verify error message includes exact dotted path and exits with non-zero code. Test both shallow and deeply nested keys.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test only verifies error message generation given a config dict and env vars, doesn't require actual file operations.

behaviors.description: XDG_CONFIG_HOME env var overrides ~/.config for config file path resolution
behaviors.justification: A unit test of path construction logic alone cannot catch failures where the constructed path is ignored or unused. Real failure: the loader reads from wrong directory despite code constructing correct path. Requires real filesystem to verify file discovery—must create config files at different locations and verify loader reads from XDG location when set, and from ~/.config when XDG_CONFIG_HOME is unset.
behaviors.test_description: Create temporary directory structure. Set XDG_CONFIG_HOME to temp dir and write config file there. Call loader and verify it reads from XDG location. In separate test, unset XDG_CONFIG_HOME and verify fallback to ~/.config. Test precedence: when both locations have files, XDG wins. Verify loader fails predictably if neither location exists.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test verifies actual file loading from correct location, not just path string construction. Filesystem interaction required to catch real failure.

behaviors.description: Environment variables with TOOL_ prefix override corresponding config file values
behaviors.justification: Tests config merging/precedence logic. No filesystem or external services. Mocking file I/O (return parsed dict) and os.environ suffice to verify that TOOL_* env vars override file values. The override logic is straightforward key-value lookup; hidden failures would be caught by checking both scalar and nested key overrides.
behaviors.test_description: Mock YAML file loading to return config dict with base values (e.g., database.host='default-host'). Set TOOL_DATABASE_HOST='prod-host' in mocked environment. Verify final config uses env var value. Test nested key mapping (TOOL_DATABASE_HOST → config['database']['host']) and partial override (file value preserved for keys without env var). Test that empty/unset env vars don't create null values.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test only needs to verify override logic given a config dict and env vars, not actual file discovery or loading.

behaviors.description: YAML syntax error includes line number where problem was detected
behaviors.justification: Tests error message formatting from YAML parsing exceptions. No filesystem needed—YAML strings parse directly without file I/O. Real library exceptions are invoked, so we verify error formatting works with actual exceptions. Mocking yaml.load() would weaken test by hiding real library behavior.
behaviors.test_description: Pass malformed YAML strings directly to config parser. For each error type (bad indentation, invalid list syntax, unclosed blocks, invalid key-value), verify error message includes correct line number and describes the problem. Use YAML strings with errors on lines 2, 5, 10 to verify accuracy. Confirm line numbers match YAML library's mark.line from exception.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test only verifies error message generation from YAML exceptions, not file I/O or system behavior.

behaviors.description: On first run, loader creates config directory and writes defaults file if missing
behaviors.justification: Filesystem operations (mkdir, file write) cannot be reliably tested with mocks. Mocking os.makedirs() or file I/O would hide real failures: directory not created, file not written, wrong permissions, or incorrect file content. Real failure: first-run directory creation silently fails or file write fails. Requires real filesystem to verify directory exists, file exists, content is correct, permissions are safe, and second run doesn't overwrite defaults.
behaviors.test_description: Use temp directory confirmed not to exist. Call loader on first run. Verify: (1) config directory created with safe permissions (mode 0o700 or similar), (2) defaults file written with correct content, (3) loader succeeds returning defaults, (4) second run does NOT overwrite existing defaults, (5) graceful handling if directory creation fails. Test cleanup: verify temp dir is safely removed.
behaviors.plan_consistent_note: Test must verify actual directory creation and file I/O, which absolutely requires filesystem interaction.
